source: Manually curated, based on I4C / cybercrime.gov.in public advisories
maintainNote: Update this list yourself over time as new scam patterns emerge. No API or account needed — just edit this file and redeploy.

patterns.explanation: ‘Digital arrest’ does not exist as a legal process in India — this phrase alone is a strong scam indicator.
Messages threatening consequences tied to your Aadhaar number are a common impersonation-scam opener.
Urgent KYC-expiry threats pushing you to click a link are a widespread phishing pattern.
Fake customs/courier seizure claims are a known fraud script used to extract payment.
Fake TRAI/telecom disconnection notices are frequently used to build urgency in scam calls.
Requests to install AnyDesk or similar remote-access apps are strongly associated with fraud that drains bank accounts.
Same pattern as AnyDesk — remote-access app requests from an unsolicited caller are a major red flag.
No bank, agency, or courier service will ever legitimately ask for your OTP.
Scammers often stage a fake uniformed video call to appear like real law enforcement.
Threats of immediate legal action over a loan/EMI are a common recovery-scam pressure tactic.
Fake courier calls claiming your parcel contains illegal items are a well-documented scam script.
Urgent SIM-block threats are commonly used to pressure victims into sharing personal details.
Fake income-tax notices sent via SMS/WhatsApp with a payment link are a known phishing tactic.
Generic urgent account-suspension threats are a broad phishing pattern across many scam scripts.
Claims of contact from CBI over phone/video call, especially demanding money, are a hallmark of digital-arrest scams.
Scammers falsely invoke ‘RBI guidelines’ or ‘RBI order’ to pressure victims into moving money to a ‘safe/verification’ account — RBI never contacts individuals this way.
Being told to transfer money to a ‘safe account’ for verification is a core digital-arrest and money-laundering-scam script — no real authority ever asks for this.
Fake claims that a parcel in your name contains narcotics, used to frighten victims into cooperating with a fraudulent ‘investigation’.
Fake courier/customs-hold messages citing FedEx, DHL, or similar international carriers are a common phishing/vishing opener worldwide, not just in India.
Messages claiming to be from the IRS (US tax authority) demanding immediate payment or threatening arrest are a well-documented scam pattern targeting NRIs and international users.
US Social Security Administration never suspends or deactivates SSNs by phone/message — this is a scripted scam opener.
Any request to pay a debt, fine, or bail using gift cards (Amazon, Google Play, iTunes, etc.) is universally a scam — no legitimate authority accepts gift cards as payment.
Unsolicited lottery/prize winnings requiring an upfront ‘processing fee’ or personal/bank details are a classic advance-fee fraud pattern.
Guaranteed or doubled returns on crypto/forex/stock investment schemes are a hallmark of Ponzi and pump-and-dump scams.
Fake job-scam pattern: paying a small fee to ‘unlock’ further paid tasks — legitimate employers never charge candidates.
Fake urgent electricity-disconnection SMS with a payment link is a widespread utility-impersonation scam pattern in India.
PAN card update/linking requests via SMS link are a common phishing pattern — official PAN updates never happen through unsolicited links.
Impersonation of army/BSF personnel (often paired with a romance-scam or online-marketplace-seller persona asking for advance payment) is a well-documented fraud pattern.
Generic ‘your customer ID/account has been blocked’ urgency messages are a broad phishing template used across many fake-bank scam scripts.
